paragraphs: I am Rudra an AI/ML engineer and graduate researcher at the University of Florida (M.S. in Artificial Intelligence Systems) with production experience across computer vision, deep learning, NLP, and MLOps. At UF's CMIL Lab (College of Medicine), I build segmentation pipelines and spatial-omics tools for kidney disease diagnostics. Before that, I shipped ML-integrated CI/CD frameworks at Jignect Technologies and deployed medical imaging models at scale.

My projects span the full breadth of applied AI: A gravitational wave detector (0.934 ROC-AUC) combining ResNet-18, Transformers, and a custom Graph Neural Network; a 9-agent RAG system with hallucination detection and self-correction; a 2.3M-parameter LLM built from scratch on the LLaMA architecture; a real-time automotive inspection system hitting 99.5% mAP@0.5 at 0.3ms inference; and a 3-pipeline LLMOps financial advisor with live Bytewax streaming. I also work across healthcare AI (DICOM parsing in Go, retinopathy screening, emotion detection), data science (RFM segmentation, churn prediction, time series), and edge AI (IoT quantization reducing inference cost by 30%). Explainability and production-readiness are non-negotiable in how I build. I am actively targeting roles in ML/AI Engineering, Applied Research, and Data Science.
